proposed_base_material_variables.material_purity: The explicitly stated overall purity level of the base metal (e.g., 99.99%), which is critical when testing non-alloyed reference materials to ensure trace elements do not artificially influence localized corrosion.

proposed_conditioned_material_variables.sample_masking_technique: The specific materials and methods (e.g., Teflon seal tape, epoxy resin undercoating) used to insulate the edges and unexposed portions of the specimen to prevent crevice corrosion artifacts during testing.
proposed_conditioned_material_variables.exposed_working_area: The explicitly defined geometric surface area of the specimen (e.g., 1 cm2) that is actively exposed to the test electrolyte during the electrochemical measurement.

proposed_experiment_variables.electrolyte_pH: The quantitatively measured or controlled pH level of the test solution, which fundamentally dictates the thermodynamic stability of passive oxides and dissolved inhibitor species.
proposed_experiment_variables.critical_pitting_temperature: The specifically extracted threshold temperature (T_pit) at which stable pitting corrosion initiates, serving as a primary and distinct measure of localized corrosion resistance alongside the pitting potential.
proposed_experiment_variables.corrosion_potential: The initial open-circuit or steady-state resting potential (E_corr) measured in the environment before the stepwise anodic polarization scan begins.
proposed_experiment_variables.inhibitor_species_and_concentration: The specific chemical identity and exact molar concentration of purposely added soluble inhibitors (e.g., Na2MoO4) designed to interact with the material and suppress pitting initiation.
